---
title: "Testing"
description: "Run unit and integration tests against the test data"
order: 60
---

Components and workflows are tested with Viash, and the CI runs the same tests on
every pull request. The [Viash guide](https://viash.io/guide/) covers how to
*write* a test; this page covers *running* them and the two test types specific
to OpenPipeline.

:::{.callout-important}
## Sync the test data first
Every test runs against the resources in `resources_test/`. If you haven't
already, fetch them as described in
[Build and test locally](development-workflow.qmd#sec-test-resources). CI uses
the same bucket, so a green run locally means a green run in CI.
:::

## Component tests

`viash test` builds the component on the fly and runs its test resource
(usually `test.py`):

```bash
viash test src/<namespace>/<component>/config.vsh.yaml   # one component
viash ns test --parallel -q <namespace>                  # a whole namespace
viash ns test --parallel                                 # the whole suite
```

## Workflow tests

Each workflow ships with a `nextflow.config` and a `test.nf` that `viash test`
runs. By convention it also carries an **`integration_test.sh`** that runs the
workflow end to end against the test data:

```bash
bash src/workflows/<category>/<workflow>/integration_test.sh
```

## Continuous integration

The same unit and integration tests run automatically on every pull request and
on every release, so there is nothing extra to trigger. Getting them green
locally before you push is the fastest way to a clean review.

**Next:** [Open a pull request →](pull-requests.qmd)
